concerns.text: The plan explicitly states GSE111322 and GSE150728 are 'Unavailable' due to missing verified URLs, leaving only GSE131907. A mixed-effects model (FR-006) with 'dataset' as a random intercept requires multiple levels (datasets) to estimate variance components. With N=1 dataset, the random effect is unidentified, rendering the statistical model invalid for its intended purpose of accounting for dataset variability.

The proposed model formula `fidelity ~ method + (1|dataset)` assumes 'method' varies within 'dataset'. However, the plan applies all three methods (PCA, t-SNE, UMAP) to the *same* dataset. If only one dataset is available, 'method' is perfectly confounded with the single level of 'dataset', making it impossible to separate method effects from dataset-specific noise. The design fails to support the causal claim of method superiority.

The plan proposes sampling 20,000 cells for t-SNE/UMAP if the dataset exceeds 50,000 cells, but computes geometric diagnostics on the 'full set (if feasible) or the sampled set'. If diagnostics are computed on the full set and embeddings on a sample, the geometric descriptors (Trustworthiness/LCA) do not characterize the manifold of the data actually embedded. This breaks the construct validity of using these diagnostics as predictors for the embedding fidelity.

The sensitivity analysis sweeps Silhouette thresholds {0.4, 0.5, 0.6} to test robustness. However, the primary clustering optimization (FR-005) maximizes Silhouette Score to *find* the optimal resolution. If the optimal resolution yields a Silhouette score outside this narrow range (e.g., 0.3 or 0.7), the sensitivity sweep tests irrelevant thresholds, failing to assess the stability of the *actual* chosen clustering solution.
